title: Tender Silky Filipino Chicken Adobo
description: A floral and silky Filipino dish made with caraway seeds, featuring a whole30-friendly and double-rise-bread adaptation. This meal-prep recipe is perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a small dry pan, toast 1 teaspoon of caraway seeds over medium heat until fragrant.
In a large Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of coconut oil over medium-high heat.
Add 2 pounds of chicken thighs to the pot and cook until browned on both sides, about 5 minutes.
Add 4 cloves of minced garlic and 2 inches of grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add 2 tablespoons of fish sauce, 4 tablespoons of coconut vinegar, and 1 teaspoon of black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Add 2 fresh bay leaves and 1 teaspoon of toasted caraway seeds to the pot and stir to combine.
Transfer the pot to the oven and braise the chicken for 25 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the chicken over 4 slices of double-rise-bread and garnish with fresh bay leaves.
Refrigerate or freeze the remaining chicken and sauce for up to 3 days.
